methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
BurnBot, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Anukool Lakhina, has raised $25M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company (LNZA) operating in the same sector. The three most direct Stage A peers—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—provide the primary anchoring points.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $25M raised as a proxy for pre-money valuation and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier to Series A progression, implies a $50M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the three same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a $150M implied valuation, with a range of $110M to $200M. The divergence between these methods reflects genuine dispersion in how climate-tech companies are valued at this stage.

Synthesizing both approaches, the analysis supports a valuation range of $42M (low) to $172M (high), with a median point of $100M. Key uncertainties include BurnBot's relative product maturity, capital efficiency, and competitive positioning versus named peers—none of which are fully determinable from available data. The wide range ($42M–$172M) reflects the meaningful variation in current Stage A Climate & Energy valuations rather than model ambiguity.
